title: Sorbus ursina
taxonomy: Sorbus ursina is classified within the family Rosaceae, which is commonly known as the rose family. This family is further divided into various subfamilies and tribes, and Sorbus ursina falls under the subfamily Amygdaloideae. The genus Sorbus, to which Sorbus ursina belongs, is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. The species is also known as the Ussuri rowan or Ussuri mountain ash. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the same family and genus.
uses: Sorbus ursina, also known as the Ussuri rowan, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Sorbus ursina, also known as the bear's sorrel or ursine rowan, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Sorbus ursina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
